titleStmt.title: Letter from John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, 10 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Robert O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943), a solicitor, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 until 1916. In 1916 he faced criticism for failing to be aware of and to prevent the outbreak of the Easter Rising on 24 April. He resigned shortly afterwards. The letter notes that O'Connell will be sending Nathan a copy of communication between O'Connell and the Archbishop of Tuam, John Healy (1841-1918), in which Healy supports the appointment of O'Connell to the position of chief crown solicitor for Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP//1916/7153/5

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
34, Kildare Street,
Dublin. 10th April 1916 Dear Sir Mathew Nathan I send you
herewith copy of a
letter of recommendation
for His Grace Revd
Dr Healy D.D. Archbishop of
of Tuam in support of
my application for the
position of Chief Crown
Solicitor for Ireland to
which I would call your
attention. If you would
wish to have the original
letter I shall be happy to
forward it to you. Believe me
Yours Sincerely John Robert O'Connell The Rt Honourable
Sir Matthew Nathan P.C.
G.C.M.G. KCKG.
The Castle Dublin
